 {"id":"bd-be7a","title":"Create npm package structure with package.json","description":"Set up initial npm package structure for @beads/bd:

## Files to create
- npm/package.json - Package metadata, dependencies, scripts
- npm/bin/bd - CLI wrapper script that invokes native binary
- npm/.gitignore - Ignore downloaded binaries
- npm/README.md - Installation and usage instructions

## package.json structure
- Name: @beads/bd (scoped package)
- Main: index.js (exports binary path)
- Bin: bin/bd (CLI entry point)
- Scripts: postinstall (download binary)
- Keywords: issue-tracker, cli, beads, bd
- License: MIT

## Bin wrapper
Simple Node.js script that:
- Spawns native binary with child_process.spawn
- Passes through all arguments and stdio
- Exits with binary's exit code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:39:47.416779-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.381258-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.381258-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-be7a","depends_on_id":"bd-febc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:40:32.923859-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tksk","title":"Support session_id field on issue close/update mutations","description":"## Context

Per Gas Town decision 009-session-events-architecture.md, work attribution for
entity CV building requires capturing which Claude Code session performed work.

## Implementation

Add session_id support to issue mutations:

### Schema
Add optional field to issue close:
- closed_by_session: string (Claude Code session UUID)

### CLI
```bash
bd close gt-abc --session "abc123-session-id"
# Or via env var:
CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=abc123 bd close gt-abc
```

### Storage
Store in issues.jsonl:
```json
{"id": "gt-abc", "status": "closed", "closed_by_session": "abc123", ...}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] bd close accepts --session flag or reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env
- [ ] session_id stored in issue close record
- [ ] bd show displays closed_by_session if present
- [ ] Works for bd update --status=closed as well

## Future

- Query by session: bd list --closed-by-session=X
- Entity chain: aggregate session activity for CV

## Related

- Gas Town docs/hop/decisions/009-session-events-architecture.md
- gt-nvz8b: gt done captures session_id","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2025-12-31T12:49:58.379182-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-31T13:13:17.590098-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T13:13:17.590098-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented session_id tracking for issue close/update mutations"}

 {"id":"bd-tm2p","title":"Polecats get stuck on interactive shell prompts (cp/mv/rm -i)","description":"During swarm operations, polecats frequently get stuck waiting for interactive prompts from shell commands like:
- cp prompting 'overwrite file? (y/n)'
- mv prompting 'overwrite file? (y/n)'  
- rm prompting 'remove file?'

This happens because macOS aliases or shell configs may have -i flags set by default.

Root cause: Claude Code runs commands that trigger interactive confirmation prompts, but cannot respond to them, causing the agent to hang indefinitely.

Observed in: Multiple polecats during GH issues swarm (Dec 2024)
- Derrick, Roustabout, Prospector, Warboy all got stuck on y/n prompts

Suggested fixes:
1. AGENTS.md should instruct agents to always use -f flag with cp/mv/rm
2. Polecat startup could set shell aliases to use non-interactive versions
3. bd prime hook could include guidance about non-interactive commands
4. Consider detecting stuck prompts and auto-recovering","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-14T16:51:24.572271-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-tmdx","title":"Investigate database pollution - unexpected issue count increases","description":"Two repositories showing unexpected issue counts:
- ~/src/beads: 280 issues (expected ~209-220)
- ~/src/dave/beads: 895 issues (675 open, 149 closed)

This suggests database pollution - issues from one repository leaking into another. Need to investigate:
1. Run bd detect-pollution on both repos
2. Check for cross-repo contamination
3. Identify source of pollution (daemon? multi-repo config? import issues?)
4. Clean up polluted databases
5. Prevent future pollution","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-06T22:50:16.957689-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T00:05:38.994405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-07T00:05:38.994405-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-wmo","title":"PruneDeletions iterates map non-deterministically","description":"## Problem

`PruneDeletions` iterates over `loadResult.Records` which is a map. Go maps iterate in random order, so:

1. `result.PrunedIDs` order is non-deterministic
2. `kept` slice order is non-deterministic → `WriteDeletions` output order varies

## Location
`internal/deletions/deletions.go:213`

## Impact
- Git diffs are noisy (file changes order on each prune)
- Tests could be flaky if they depend on order
- Harder to debug/audit

## Fix
Sort by ID or timestamp before iterating:

```go
// Convert map to slice and sort
var records []DeletionRecord
for _, r := range loadResult.Records {
    records = append(records, r)
}
sort.Slice(records, func(i, j int) bool {
    return records[i].ID < records[j].ID
})
```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:49:11.290916-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:15:21.903649-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:15:21.903649-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-wp2q5","title":"Review PR #1010: Add FreeBSD documentation","description":"dispatched_by: beads/crew/emma

Review and approve PR #1010 from jordanhubbard that adds FreeBSD support documentation.

PR URL: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/pull/1010

## Summary
This PR clarifies installation docs to include FreeBSD alongside Linux, adding FreeBSD-specific install notes.

## Changes (57 additions, 5 deletions)
- .goreleaser.yml: Add FreeBSD to quick install text
- README.md: Add FreeBSD to requirements
- docs/INSTALLING.md: Add FreeBSD section
- website/docs/getting-started/installation.md: Add FreeBSD section
- website/docs/intro.md: Add FreeBSD to quick install
- website/static/llms-full.txt: Corresponding updates

## Review Tasks
1. Verify the install.sh script actually works on FreeBSD (or at least doesn't break)
2. Check that documentation is consistent across all files
3. Validate with: make test
4. If all good, approve the PR with: gh pr review 1010 --repo steveyegge/beads --approve
5. After approval, merge with: gh pr merge 1010 --repo steveyegge/beads --squash

## Why Easy-Win
- Documentation-only change
- No code changes
- Author is jordanhubbard (BSD expert)
- Follows existing patterns for Linux docs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:01:28.525277-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:03:26.309302-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:03:26.309302-08:00","close_reason":"PR #1010 reviewed, approved, and merged. FreeBSD documentation added consistently across all install docs."}
